{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Sales Executive",
  "description": "Smartflow Technologies Limited is Nigeria&#39;s foremost Flow and Level Automation company, delivering premium and yet cost-effective solutions to clients with unparalleled professionalism and skill. In addition to being an engineering-focused organization, Smartflow Technologies is involved in the supply, installation, and support of fuelling systems and equipment, including but not limited to Pumps and Dispensers.&nbsp;

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Senior Sales Executive

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Sales Executive to drive business growth by identifying new opportunities, building strong client relationships, and promoting the company&#39;s engineering solutions.
	The role involves marketing our flow and level automation solutions, fuelling systems, pumps, dispensers, and related products and services to prospective and existing clients across various industries. The ideal candidate must have previous experience in the Oil, Gas &amp; Mining Sector.


Responsibilities


	Develop and achieve the sales volume/revenue goals as defined in the business plan
	Generate &amp; close leads by identifying potential clients, engaging prospects, presenting solutions, and finalizing deals to achieve sales targets.
	Tracking key sales metrics, analysing performance, and preparing timely and accurate reports for management.
	Creating and sending proposals, quotes, and other sales documentation to clients.
	Building and maintaining strong relationships with both new and existing clients to foster trust and loyalty.
	Maintaining accurate and organized records of sales activities, client interactions, and leads in the CRM system.
	Collaborating with other departments to ensure seamless communication, align sales strategies with business objectives, and provide comprehensive support for customer needs and service delivery.
	Coordinate with the Marketing Team to ensure strategic marketing awareness and marketing initiatives across the region.
	Developing initiatives to push sales and product awareness of the company&rsquo;s products and services.
	Conduct regular market visits to check route coverage and availability of company products in the market, competitors&rsquo; activities, and look for new business opportunities.
	Attending industry events, conferences, and trade shows to enhance brand visibility, network, and generate leads.
	Coordinate with the OHS coordinator and investigate all reported incidents (near-misses and accidents).


Requirements


	Candidate must possess a B.Sc. or HND in any field
	3 -5 years of sales experience in the oil and gas sector or B2B sales experience
	Existing customer base in the relevant industry is an advantage.
	Willing to travel around Northern Nigeria 40%
	Must have very good knowledge of the Northern region.
	Candidate must reside in Abuja
	Must have a good understanding of Excel and other Microsoft Office packages
	Good written and verbal communication skills
	Knowledge of occupational health and safety.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: careers@smartflowtech.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis; we encourage early applications as we are hiring immediately.",

Senior Sales Executive Job at Smartflow Technologies Limited




        
    

        
        Smartflow Technologies Limited is Nigeria's foremost Flow and Level Automation company, delivering premium and yet cost-effective solutions to clients with unparalleled professionalism and skill. In addition to being an engineering-focused organization, Smartflow Technologies is involved in the supply, installation, and support of fuelling systems and equipment, including but not limited to Pumps and Dispensers. 

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Senior Sales Executive

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a proactive and results-driven Sales Executive to drive business growth by identifying new opportunities, building strong client relationships, and promoting the company's engineering solutions.
	The role involves marketing our flow and level automation solutions, fuelling systems, pumps, dispensers, and related products and services to prospective and existing clients across various industries. The ideal candidate must have previous experience in the Oil, Gas & Mining Sector.


Responsibilities


	Develop and achieve the sales volume/revenue goals as defined in the business plan
	Generate & close leads by identifying potential clients, engaging prospects, presenting solutions, and finalizing deals to achieve sales targets.
	Tracking key sales metrics, analysing performance, and preparing timely and accurate reports for management.
	Creating and sending proposals, quotes, and other sales documentation to clients.
	Building and maintaining strong relationships with both new and existing clients to foster trust and loyalty.
	Maintaining accurate and organized records of sales activities, client interactions, and leads in the CRM system.
	Collaborating with other departments to ensure seamless communication, align sales strategies with business objectives, and provide comprehensive support for customer needs and service delivery.
	Coordinate with the Marketing Team to ensure strategic marketing awareness and marketing initiatives across the region.
	Developing initiatives to push sales and product awareness of the company’s products and services.
	Conduct regular market visits to check route coverage and availability of company products in the market, competitors’ activities, and look for new business opportunities.
	Attending industry events, conferences, and trade shows to enhance brand visibility, network, and generate leads.
	Coordinate with the OHS coordinator and investigate all reported incidents (near-misses and accidents).


Requirements


	Candidate must possess a B.Sc. or HND in any field
	3 -5 years of sales experience in the oil and gas sector or B2B sales experience
	Existing customer base in the relevant industry is an advantage.
	Willing to travel around Northern Nigeria 40%
	Must have very good knowledge of the Northern region.
	Candidate must reside in Abuja
	Must have a good understanding of Excel and other Microsoft Office packages
	Good written and verbal communication skills
	Knowledge of occupational health and safety.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their applications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

Note: Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis; we encourage early applications as we are hiring immediately.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Marketing Officer Job at Adin Open University (AOU)




        
    

        
        Adin Open University (AOU) was conceived to widen access to high-quality higher education through thoughtful use of technology, flexible delivery and strong learner support. Our model combines guided online learning, experienced faculty, digital resources, practical assessment and meaningful connections to industry and society. We are a forward-thinking institution committed to excellence in Open, Distance, and e-Learning. Our Mission is to provide accessible, affordable and technology-enabled education that develops knowledge, skills, enterprise and responsible leadership.
     

            
    



Applications are invited from suitably qualified, experienced, passionate, and innovative professionals who are ready to contribute to a technology-driven learning environment and help shape the future of higher education in Nigeria for appointment into the position below:

Job Position: Marketing Officer

Job Location: Ubulu-Unor, Delta
Employment Type: Full-time

Requirements
Applicants must:


	Possess qualifications and professional certifications appropriate to this position.
	Have current teaching, research, administrative, or professional experience in a recognized university or higher institution.
	Demonstrate strong ICT proficiency and technology-driven work competencies.
	Must have experience in Open, Distance, and e-Learning environments.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should their applications electronically to "The Secretary,
ADIN Open University, Nigeria" via: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

Application Documents
Interested candidates should submit the following:


	Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV)
	Letter of Application indicating the position applied for
	Copies of Academic and Professional Credentials
	One Recent Passport Photograph (White Background).
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "title": "Internal Control Checker",
  "description": "Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company&#39;s flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Checker

Job Location: Northern Nigeria

Purpose


	To support effective inventory control processes by monitoring product movement, verifying inventory transactions, and ensuring compliance with internal control procedures to minimize wastage, prevent losses, and maintain inventory accuracy.


The Job


	Monitor and initiate the loading process for company products, ensuring compliance with approved loading procedures.
	Detect and prevent overloading and underloading of company products.
	Verify that invoiced products correspond with gate passes and weighbridge dockets and maintain accurate load-out records.
	Observe the discharge of AGO, petrol, and gas deliveries to ensure the correct quantity and quality are received.
	Monitor the loading of scrap materials and ensure only authorized items are released for collection.
	Inspect locally supplied goods and materials to confirm accuracy and quality before utilization and payment.
	Monitor weighbridge operations to ensure compliance with approved Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).Verify invoiced goods and goods receipt transactions on D365.


?Qualification?s


	5 O-Level credits, including Mathematics and English/ NABTEB, or Trade Test Grade.
	NABTEB / City &amp; Guilds (C&amp;G) in a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of two (2) years of relevant experience in inventory control, warehouse operations, logistics, manufacturing, finance operations, or a related field.


The Person Must:


	Possess strong attention to detail and a high level of accuracy.
	Have good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Be able to identify discrepancies and escalate issues promptly.
	Possess good record-keeping and documentation skills.

Internal Control Checker Job at Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc




        
    

        
        Flour Mills of Nigeria Plc has been a part of the lives of Nigerians at home and abroad. Our global vision is to be a leading foods company in Africa providing high quality and affordable products in the most convenient ways to consumers through world class brands like Golden Penny Flour, Golden Penny Semovita, Goldenvita, Golden Pasta and Golden Noodles. The Company's flagship brand, Golden Penny, remains one of the best known and the preferred brands amongst bakers, confectioneries and consumers in Nigeria.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Internal Control Checker

Job Location: Northern Nigeria

Purpose
     

            
    




	To support effective inventory control processes by monitoring product movement, verifying inventory transactions, and ensuring compliance with internal control procedures to minimize wastage, prevent losses, and maintain inventory accuracy.


The Job


	Monitor and initiate the loading process for company products, ensuring compliance with approved loading procedures.
	Detect and prevent overloading and underloading of company products.
	Verify that invoiced products correspond with gate passes and weighbridge dockets and maintain accurate load-out records.
	Observe the discharge of AGO, petrol, and gas deliveries to ensure the correct quantity and quality are received.
	Monitor the loading of scrap materials and ensure only authorized items are released for collection.
	Inspect locally supplied goods and materials to confirm accuracy and quality before utilization and payment.
	Monitor weighbridge operations to ensure compliance with approved Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs).Verify invoiced goods and goods receipt transactions on D365.


?Qualification?s


	5 O-Level credits, including Mathematics and English/ NABTEB, or Trade Test Grade.
	NABTEB / City & Guilds (C&G) in a related discipline.


Experience:


	Minimum of two (2) years of relevant experience in inventory control, warehouse operations, logistics, manufacturing, finance operations, or a related field.


The Person Must:


	Possess strong attention to detail and a high level of accuracy.
	Have good analytical and problem-solving skills.
	Be able to identify discrepancies and escalate issues promptly.
	Possess good record-keeping and documentation skills.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Commercial & Marketing Manager",
  "description": "DropX is building an AI?assisted last?mile delivery network and marketplace (e-commerce infrastructure) in Lagos. Our unwavering commitment to innovation, quality, and excellence is reflected in every facet of our operations.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Commercial &amp; Marketing Manager
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	The Marketing &amp; Commercial Manager will be responsible for driving customer acquisition, brand growth, strategic partnerships, revenue generation, and market expansion initiatives.
	The ideal candidate is a commercially minded professional with strong marketing expertise and a proven track record of delivering business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute marketing and commercial strategies to drive revenue growt
	Identify and secure strategic partnerships with e-commerce businesses, merchants, and key stakeholders.
	Lead customer acquisition, retention, a- Experience in logistics, e-commerce, technology, supply chain or related industries is highly desirable.
	Strong understanding of customer acquisition strategies, partnership development, and revenue growth.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work independently in a fast-paced startup environment.
	Collaborate with operations and leadership teams to align commercial objectives with business goals.
	Monitor performance metrics and prepare reports to support data-driven decision-making.


Requirements


	&nbsp;Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Professional certifications are an added advantage.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Marketing, Business Development, Commercial Management, Sales, or a related field.
	Experience in logistics, e-commerce, technology, supply chain or related industries is highly desirable.
	Strong understanding of customer acquisition strategies, partnership development, and revenue growth.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to work independently in a fast-paced startup environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: dropx.hr@dropx.africa and copy: kola@dropx.africa using &quot;Marketing &amp; Commercial Manager&quot; as the subject of the email.",

Commercial & Marketing Manager Job at DropX




        
    

        
        DropX is building an AI?assisted last?mile delivery network and marketplace (e-commerce infrastructure) in Lagos. Our unwavering commitment to innovation, quality, and excellence is reflected in every facet of our operations.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Commercial & Marketing Manager
Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Marketing & Commercial Manager will be responsible for driving customer acquisition, brand growth, strategic partnerships, revenue generation, and market expansion initiatives.
	The ideal candidate is a commercially minded professional with strong marketing expertise and a proven track record of delivering business growth.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop and execute marketing and commercial strategies to drive revenue growt
	Identify and secure strategic partnerships with e-commerce businesses, merchants, and key stakeholders.
	Lead customer acquisition, retention, a- Experience in logistics, e-commerce, technology, supply chain or related industries is highly desirable.
	Strong understanding of customer acquisition strategies, partnership development, and revenue growth.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to  work independently in a fast-paced startup environment.
	Collaborate with operations and leadership teams to align commercial objectives with business goals.
	Monitor performance metrics and prepare reports to support data-driven decision-making.


Requirements


	 Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Economics, or a related discipline.
	Professional certifications are an added advantage.
	Minimum of 5 years of experience in Marketing, Business Development, Commercial Management, Sales, or a related field.
	Experience in logistics, e-commerce, technology, supply chain or related industries is highly desirable.
	Strong understanding of customer acquisition strategies, partnership development, and revenue growth.
	Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management skills.
	Ability to work independently in a fast-paced startup environment.


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and copy: [email protected] using "Marketing & Commercial Manager" as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Oil & Gas Reliability Engineer III",
  "description": "Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil &amp; gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities &acirc;&euro;&ldquo; with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Oil &amp; Gas Reliability Engineer III

Job Location: Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

Job Description


	We are seeking an experienced Oil &amp; Gas Reliability Engineer III to drive asset reliability, optimize equipment performance, and improve production availability across our operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead reliability improvement initiatives and asset performance optimization.
	Analyze equipment and unit performance data to identify reliability threats and improvement opportunities.
	Manage production availability threats and maintain the threats database.
	Benchmark equipment performance against industry standards and similar facilities.
	Support lifecycle asset management, risk mitigation, and loss elimination strategies.
	Serve as a subject matter expert on cause-and-effect methodology and reliability best practices.
	Resolve complex technical issues and provide expert reliability guidance.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Engineering or equivalent professional experience.
	Proven experience in reliability engineering within the oil and gas industry.
	Strong knowledge of asset reliability, maintenance strategies, and lifecycle asset management.
	Advanced analytical and data manipulation skills.
	Ability to work independently and provide technical leadership on complex engineering challenges.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Demonstrated innovation, business acumen, and a track record of delivering high-value technical solutions.


Method of application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hesprojectrecruitment@gmail.com using &quot;Oil &amp; Gas Reliability Engineer III&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Oil & Gas Reliability Engineer III Job at Habrams Energy




        
    

        
        Habrams Energy offers you engineering, procurement, construction management, operations, and maintenance services for oil & gas projects in the Gulf of Guinea. We provide specialist solutions to international oil companies, marginal field operators, and service companies.

Our work ranges from retrofitting and de-bottlenecking to designing complete facilities â€“ with seamless transition from construction to operations.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Oil & Gas Reliability Engineer III

Job Location: Akwa Ibom
Employment Type: Full-time 

Job Description
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced Oil & Gas Reliability Engineer III to drive asset reliability, optimize equipment performance, and improve production availability across our operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Lead reliability improvement initiatives and asset performance optimization.
	Analyze equipment and unit performance data to identify reliability threats and improvement opportunities.
	Manage production availability threats and maintain the threats database.
	Benchmark equipment performance against industry standards and similar facilities.
	Support lifecycle asset management, risk mitigation, and loss elimination strategies.
	Serve as a subject matter expert on cause-and-effect methodology and reliability best practices.
	Resolve complex technical issues and provide expert reliability guidance.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Engineering or equivalent professional experience.
	Proven experience in reliability engineering within the oil and gas industry.
	Strong knowledge of asset reliability, maintenance strategies, and lifecycle asset management.
	Advanced analytical and data manipulation skills.
	Ability to work independently and provide technical leadership on complex engineering challenges.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite.
	Demonstrated innovation, business acumen, and a track record of delivering high-value technical solutions.


Method of application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Oil & Gas Reliability Engineer III" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
